description:
Goal: fix compiler2's original source-layer omission by making grouped quoted source a first-class, stable thing in the source heap. A logical function surface (`@doc`/`@spec` + multi-clause `fn`/`fnp`/`defmacro`, or `extern`) should be represented as one rooted quoted value in the same Fz heap, not as a Rust-side grouping convention hidden behind old parser `FnDef` authority.

Grounding:
- `src/compiler2/quoted_surface.rs` can currently detect that multiple quoted items belong to one logical function surface, but it does not yet have a first-class grouped quoted root to carry that surface.
- The current fallback is to clone the old parser's `FnDef`, which hides the missing source-layer representation and keeps the old parser authoritative for grouped clauses, attrs, and protocol-impl callback surfaces.
- Naively rebuilding a synthetic grouped list root on every read would change `{heap, root}` identity and spuriously churn `FunctionDefined`/downstream revisions even when the logical grouped surface is unchanged.
- Elixir's macro/compiler model works because parser, compiler, and macros stay in one term world. The concordant fz2 move is to compose/intern grouped quoted roots in the same Fz heap instead of escaping to a second AST world.
- Compiler services and macro-produced definitions will need this same substrate: `Fz.Compiler.def` should be able to publish a grouped quoted function source without inventing a parallel representation.

Signal:
- `QuotedSourceHeap` / `QuotedSourceBuilder` can compose and intern stable grouped quoted roots from existing item roots inside the owning heap.
- Re-reading the same code/module/protocol-impl body yields the same `{heap, root}` for the same logical grouped function surface.
- Grouped function source carries attached attrs plus all clauses (or an `extern`) as one quoted root; protocol-impl callbacks use the same representation.
- `quoted_surface` / source readers no longer need old parser `FnDef` authority to recover grouped function surface that the source layer failed to represent.
- Tests cover multi-clause grouping, attr attachment, repeated-read root stability, and protocol-impl callback grouping.

Change:
- add first-class heap-level quoted composition/interning APIs for grouped source lists in `src/compiler2/source.rs`
- teach `src/compiler2/quoted_surface.rs` and the source readers to emit grouped function/callback carriers as real quoted roots in that heap
- route protocol-impl callback discovery through that same grouped source representation instead of walking parser `Item::Fn` lists as authority
- update quoted-source docs with the grouped-root/root-stability invariant

Why:
- this fixes the problem at the right layer, not downstream
- once grouped quoted source is real and stable, the remaining `legacy_fn_def` derivation becomes a narrow transitional artifact instead of a hidden second source authority
- the same facility should serve ordinary defs, protocol callbacks, and later macro/compiler-service-produced definitions
title: Compiler2 quoted source composition: make grouped function roots first-class and stable
